content.text:
The search is thorough and conclusive. There is exactly one bug in this codebase.

---

## Off-by-One Bug Found

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461914985-h5cx4y/src/utils.ts`
**Line:** 7

### The Bug

```ts
// Lines 1–11 of src/utils.ts
/**
 * Process items by converting each to uppercase.
 * Known issue: users report the last item is never processed.
 */
export function processItems(items: string[]): string[] {
  const results: string[] = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {   // <-- BUG on line 7
    results.push(items[i].toUpperCase());
  }
  return results;
}
```

### What is wrong

The loop condition is `i < items.length - 1`. For an array of N elements (valid indices 0 through N-1), this loop stops at index N-2, meaning the last element (`items[N-1]`) is never visited or pushed into `results`. The comment above the function even acknowledges the symptom: *"users report the last item is never processed."*

### The fix

Change the loop condition from:
```ts
i < items.length - 1
```
to:
```ts
i < items.length
```

### No other issues found

All other searched patterns across JS, TS, JSX, TSX, Python, Go, Ruby, Java, Go, Swift, Kotlin, C, C++, Rust, and C# files returned no matches. This is the sole off-by-one defect in the codebase.
